skill.name: graphql-schema
skill.description: Guides creation of GraphQL queries, mutations, fragments, Apollo Client hooks, and generated TypeScript operation files.
skill.summary: Create typed GraphQL operations with Apollo Client codegen patterns and mutation handling.
skill.author: ChrisWiles

security_audit.summary: Static high-risk detections are mostly false positives from Markdown formatting, GraphQL field names, and code examples. The confirmed concern is legitimate but contextual: the skill recommends running repository-defined npm codegen scripts, which can execute untrusted project code.

medium_findings.title: Project Codegen Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run npm codegen scripts such as gql:typegen and sync-types. This is a legitimate GraphQL workflow, but npm scripts execute repository-defined code and should be reviewed before use in untrusted projects.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npm commands are explicit and semantically relevant to code generation. The risk depends on the target repository scripts, so it is elevated but not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, system-reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick detections occur in Markdown prose, code fences, or GraphQL field examples. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, host reconnaissance, prompt injection, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible as documentation examples and GraphQL fields. There is no executable Ruby code, no shell backtick execution, and no suspicious instruction override text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository npm Script Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: Commands shown in the skill delegate execution to npm scripts from the current project. This pattern is useful for GraphQL generation but can execute arbitrary package scripts if the project is untrusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are clear, but their behavior is determined outside this skill by project package scripts. That makes the concern contextual rather than confirmed malware.

content.value_statement: GraphQL frontends often drift between operations, generated types, and UI error states. This skill gives repeatable patterns for .gql files, Apollo generated hooks, and safe mutation flows.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to place GraphQL queries, mutations, fragments, and generated files.
Guides creation of separate .gql operation files instead of inline gql literals.
Demonstrates Apollo generated hook usage for queries and mutations.
Defines mutation requirements for loading, success, disabled, and error states.
Explains common Apollo fetch policies and query options.
Lists project codegen commands for generating GraphQL types.
limitations: Assumes a frontend project that uses Apollo Client and generated hooks.
Does not design server schemas, resolvers, authorization, or database models.
Requires project-specific npm scripts to exist before codegen commands work.
Examples are patterns and must be adapted to local component and import conventions.

use_cases.title: Add a Typed Query
use_cases.description: Create a .gql query file, generate types, and wire the generated hook into a list or detail view.

use_cases.title: Standardize Mutation UX
use_cases.description: Apply consistent loading, disabled, success, and error handling around Apollo mutation triggers.

use_cases.title: Review GraphQL Client Patterns
use_cases.description: Check a code review for inline operations, missing generated hooks, and incomplete mutation feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new list or detail query.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GraphQL query pattern for this component. Use a separate .gql file, generated Apollo hook usage, and clear loading, error, and empty states.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Mutation Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a form or action mutation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GraphQL mutation flow for this user action. Include the .gql operation, generated hook usage, disabled state, loading state, success feedback, and onError handling.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Inline Operations
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of existing Apollo code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component for GraphQL anti-patterns. Move inline gql operations into .gql files and explain the generated hook changes needed.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Typed GraphQL Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a team workflow for GraphQL operations in this repository. Cover file placement, fragments, codegen commands, mutation UX requirements, and review checks.

output_examples.input: I need a typed query for an item list with pagination.
output: A suggested .gql file location near the list component.
A query structure with pagination variables and essential item fields described in prose.
A generated hook usage outline with loading, error, empty, and populated states.
A reminder to review and run the project codegen script.

output_examples.input: I need a create-item mutation with good UI behavior.
output: A generated mutation hook flow with success and error feedback.
Button behavior requirements for disabled and loading states.
Cache update guidance when the created item should appear immediately.

output_examples.input: Review this Apollo component for GraphQL mistakes.
output: A list of issues such as inline operations, missing generated hooks, or absent error handling.
A migration plan using .gql files and generated types.
Follow-up checks for codegen and mutation user feedback.

best_practices: Review package scripts before running codegen commands in an unfamiliar repository.
Keep GraphQL operations in .gql files and use generated hooks in components.
Require every mutation trigger to handle loading, disabled, success, and error states.
anti_patterns: Do not inline gql literals directly inside React components.
Do not call mutations without an onError handler and user-facing failure feedback.
Do not use optimistic updates for destructive or validation-heavy operations.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a GraphQL server schema?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on client-side GraphQL operations, generated hooks, and Apollo Client usage patterns.

faq.question: Can it work without Apollo Client?
faq.answer: The documented examples assume Apollo Client and generated hooks. Other clients need adaptation.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill recommends npm codegen commands. These commands are normal, but repository scripts can execute arbitrary local code.

faq.question: Are the static high-risk findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are false positives from Markdown and GraphQL examples.

faq.question: What should users check before running codegen?
faq.answer: They should inspect the project package scripts and dependencies, especially in untrusted repositories.

faq.question: Does the skill include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Only briefly. It mentions using related testing patterns for generated hook mocks.
